Its sound in Arabic is that of th in the English thin; but in Hindūstānī it has the sound of s. According to the abjad, q.v., it has the numerical value of 500.
s.m. White (the colour); — (f. -ī) a hawk, a falcon.
v.t. corr. of sewnā or seʼonā, q.v. v.t. To attend on, serve; to adore, worship; to heed, give attention to; to protect, watch over, guard; to brood, incubate, rear, hatch, sit on (eggs)
subs: To sit on eggs, to brood; (fig.) to mope.

ety: prob. fr. simaṭnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Tongs (i.q. ćimṭā).

v.t. = sūṅthnā, q.v.; — s.m. = sūthnā; and syūṅṭhā, qq.v. v.t. To rub down; to rub (the body), to shampoo; to strip the leaves off a branch, or off vegetables, &c. (by running the hand down it or them); to strip, lay bare; to draw or unsheath (a sword); to straighten (a wire, &c., by rubbing the hand down it); to throw out, bale out (water); to drain, exhaust.
s.m. A tail, the breast; the womb; the hip or flank; a warrior; a thief; a wicked man; an outcast, a barbarian; a jewel; nectar; a tree; — passing, crossing.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit -इत (-ita).

ety: Z. yatha; S. यथा
def: adv. To, until, as far as; as long as, whilst; even to; in order (that), to the end (that); in such a manner (that); — intj. Look! behold! take care! beware!
subs: adv. How far? whither? how long? till when?
adv. Till what time? to what extent? to what length? when? how much? how many?
adv. How many? how long? by how much?
adv., How many times? how often?
adv. Up to the present time, till now, hitherto, yet
tā-kě, adv. So that, in order that, to the end that; as long as, until, so long; — whither?
tā-ba-maqdūr, adv. To the extent of (one's) power, to the utmost or the best of (one's) ability, as much as possible (syn. ḥattaʼl-maqdūr; maqdūr-bhar)
conj. Yet, nevertheless, still ( = tau bhī)
adv. Yet, still, hitherto, till now; till.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تا (tā), from Middle Persian [script needed] (OD /⁠tā⁠/, “until, so that”), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎠𐎫𐎠 (y-a-t-a /⁠yātā⁠/).

r: ṭa, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, t, true, ṭa-kār
def: s.m. The letter or the sound ṭ
subs: vulg. ṭa-barg, s.m. The cerebral class of letters in the Nāgarī alphabet.

def: insep. prep., By (occurring only in the oath تالله taʼllāhi 'by God').
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit -इत (-ita).

ety: Prk. तेहि, तेवहि, तिवहि (hi being the gen. or obl. suff., and omitted in Hindī); S. तावत्
def: Inflected bases (in Braj) of the sing. of the pers. pron. of the third person, corresponding to the Hindī us; tis; 'him, her, it' (e.g. tā-kau = us-kā; tā-kī = tis-kī; tā-te or tā-teṅ = tis-se).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تا (tā), from Middle Persian [script needed] (OD /⁠tā⁠/, “until, so that”), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎠𐎫𐎠 (y-a-t-a /⁠yātā⁠/).

def: s.m. Exertion, labour, toil, trouble, hardship; fatigue, weariness, lassitude

ety: inf. n. v of عبد 'to serve, obey, &c.'
def: s.m. Applying oneself to acts of devotion; being pious; devotion.

ety: inf. n. v of عدو 'to exceed, transgress,' &c.
def: s.f. Wrong-doing, injustice, tyranny, oppression, injury, violence, force, compulsion, extortion, exaction; (in Gram.) being or becoming transitive (a verb); causation.

ety: inf. n. v of عدّ
def: s.m. Exceeding in number; plurality; numbering; number; multiplying.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَدُّد (ta'addud), from Arabic تَعَدُّد (taʕaddud).

ety: inf. n. v of عفن 'to become putrid'
def: s.m. Stink, fetor, fetidness.

ety: inf. n. v of عہد 'to contract, covenant,' &c.
def: s.m. Agreement, covenant, contract, engagement, lease; rent
subs: s.m. The holder of an agreement, or contract, or lease.

ety: inf. n. v of اهل 'to marry,' &c.
def: s.m. Marrying; founding a family.

ety: inf. n. v of عيل 'to be poor'
def: s.m. Land held by a member of a royal family (especially applied to the royal family of Dehli), a royal appanage

ety: inf. n. v of عين 'to eye,' &c.
def: s.m. Specifying, fixing, determining, assigning, appointing, deputing, establishing; appointment, establishment, determination, &c.
subs: v.t. To specify; to fix, determine, establish, assign, appoint, institute, depute, &c.

ety: pl. of taʻaiyun; but also used in the sing.
def: s.m. Appointments; appointment, service, duty; a garrison
subs: v.t. To appoint, depute, to send on duty; to enlist; to put in possession
v.n. To be appointed, be deputed, be sent on duty.

def: s.f. Appointment, service, duty; taking up the duties of an appointment; the object or business of an appointment, tour of service; a deputation; a detachment of troops; a guard; a garrison; any body of men told off for a special duty.

ety: inf. n. v of عيش 'to live'
def: s.m. Procuring a livelihood by labour and industry, labouring for a living; — living a pleasant life; rejoicing.

ety: inf. n. v. of عجب 'to wonder'
def: s.m. Wondering (at); wonder, astonishment, surprise, amazement; admiration
subs: (-par), To wonder (at), be astonished (at); to deem strange or extraordinary; to admire.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَجُّب (ta'ajjub), borrowed from Arabic تَعَجُّب (taʕajjub). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi تعجب (t'jb).

ety: inf. n. v of اخر 'to keep or hold back,' &c.
def: s.m. Delay, &c. (see tāḵẖīr); — part. adj. Retarded. postponed; following.

ety: inf. n. v of عقب 'to follow'
def: s.m. Searching (after), tracking, pursuing; tracing (intelligence, &c.); — punishment, chastisement.

ety: inf. n. v of عقل 'to be or become intelligent'
def: s.m. Understanding, perceiving; informing.

ety: inf. n. vi of عقب 'to come after'
def: s.m. Following, pursuit; persecution (syn. pīćhā); alternating, alternation, alternate succession.
(imperat. of تعالي taʻālā, q.v.), intj., lit. 'Be exalted'; — taʻāl-taʻal, taʻalaʼllāh, 'God be exalted! Good God! Bravo!

ety: 3rd per. sing. perf. vi of علو 'to be high'
def: adj., lit. 'He is exalted'; or 'be He exalted'; the Exalted, the Most High (God).

ety: inf. n. v of علو 'to be high'
def: s.f. Becoming high or elevated; highness, eminence; exalting ooneself, appearing conspicuous.

ety: inf. n. v of علق 'to hang or cling (to)'
def: s.m. Being attached (to), being connected (to or with), pertaining (to), dependent (on); attachment; dependance; connexion, relation; relationship; concern, reference, regard; consideration, reflection; commerce, correspondence; means of support, employment, situation, office; property, possession; a manor, &c. = taʻalluqa, q.v.
subs: (-se), To be connected (with), to relate (to); to depend (on); to have reference (to), to concern, to pertain (to); — to have an interest (in).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَلُّق (ta'alluq), borrowed from Arabic تَعَلُّق (taʕalluq), from تَعَلَّقَ (taʕallaqa). First attested in c. 1582 as Middle Hindi تعلق (t'lq /⁠ta'alluq⁠/).

ety: for A. تعلقة, taʻalluqat, fem.
def: s.m. Connexion, relationship ( = taʻalluq); possession (of land); fee, manor, lordship, estate; a division of a province, a district
subs: s.m. One who holds a taʻalluqa, a landholder, possessor of an estate, lord of a manor, feoffee
s.f. The tenure, or the status, of a taʻalluq-dār; the estate of a taʻalluq-dār.

def: s.m. pl. (of taʻalluqa), Lordships (of land), manors, estates, &c.

ety: of taʻalluq
def: s.m. Worldly concerns.

ety: inf. n. v of علّ 'to use for the purpose of diverting'
def: s.m. 'Diverting oneself'; whiling away time (with); occupying oneself vainly; making an excuse; procrastination; excuse; objection.

ety: inf. n. v of الم 'to be in pain'
def: s.m. Pain, torment; grief, sorrow; melancholy.

ety: inf. n. v of علم 'to know'
def: s.m. Being taught, learning; study; knowledge.

ety: inf. n. of طعم 'to eat'
def: s.m. Eating; — food, victuals
subs: s.m. A large spoon to serve out food (rice, &c.) with, a ladle
wety: Borrowed from Arabic طَعَام (ṭaʕām).

ety: inf. n. v of عمق 'to be deep'
def: s.m. Diving or going deep (into); penetrating; deepness; penetration; profoundness.

ety: inf. n. v of امل 'to hope for'
def: s.m. Careful consideration, meditation, reflection, deliberation; deliberate action, pausing, hesitation, cautious procedure; disinclination, scruple
subs: (-meṅ), To consider carefully, to reflect, deliberate; to act or proceed with deliberation (in), to pause, to hesitate, &c.

ety: inf. n. v of اني 'to draw near'
def: s.f. Waiting; delay, procrastination.

ety: inf. n. v of انس 'to be familiar,' &c.
def: s.m. Acquaintance; familiarity; becoming domesticated and tame (as an animal).

ety: inf. n. v of عرف 'to know'
def: s.m. Inquiring with the view to learn, seeking knowledge; acquainting oneself (with), learning; instructing, notifying.

ety: inf. n. v of عرض 'to present or offer itself (a thing)'
def: s.m. Happening, occurring; coming before, presenting oneself (or itself); noticing; opposing, hindering; opposition, hindrance, obstacle, impediment. (A. pl. taʻarruẓāt.)

ety: inf. n. vi of عرف 'to know'
def: s.m. Knowing one another; mutual acquaintance; — rule, fashion, custom.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَارُف (ta'āruf), borrowed from Arabic تَعَارُف (taʕāruf), from تَعَارَفَ (taʕārafa).

ety: inf. n. v of عشق 'to love'
def: s.m. Exhibiting or showing love, falling in love (with), being in love; love, affection
subs: v.n. To make a show of love or affection

ety: inf. n. v of عصب 'to cling to; to gather round'
def: s.m. Zeal (for a party, or cause); prejudice (for or against); bigotry, religious persecution; superstition.

ety: inf. n. v of اسف 'to grieve'
def: s.m. Grieving, lamenting, pining, brooding over trouble or affliction; grief, regret, repentance ( = afsos)
subs: (-se), To feel regret (for), to sigh (over); to despair (of).

ety: acc. of taʼassuf
def: adv. With or in grief or regret; remorsefully.

ety: inf. n. v of اثر 'to make a mark,' &c.
def: s.m. Becoming marked or impressed, receiving an impression; impression; penetration.

ety: for A. طاعة, quasi-inf. n. of iv of طوع 'to become submissive,' &c.
def: s.f. Obedience, submission, submissiveness; devotion; obsequiousness.
s.f. pl. (of t̤āʻat, q.v.), Acts of devotion, &c.

ety: inf. n. vi of عطو 'to take'
def: s.f. Taking with the hand; giving reciprocal presents; mutual giving or surrender; a silent kind of bargain, the vendor handing over the thing to the buyer, and the latter giving him the price without speaking.

ety: inf. n. v of عطل 'to be destitute (of)'
def: s.m. Being without bracelets or other ornaments (a woman); being or remaining without work or occupation; idleness; being void or vacant (a place, or tent, &c.).
s.m. Assistance; mutual aid; conspiring.

ety: inf. n. v of عظم 'to be large, great,' &c.
def: s.m. Magnifying oneself, behaving proudly or haughtily; pride, haughtiness, arrogance, insolence; — magnificence, grandeur, majesty.

ety: inf. n. v of عذر 'to excuse'
def: s.m. Adducing or urging an excuse; excuse, apology.

ety: Pehl. tāp; Z. tāpa; S. ताप, rt. तप्
def: s.f. Heat, warmth; burning, inflaming; pain, affliction, grief; anger, indignation, wrath, rage; light, radiance, lustre, splendour; strength, power, ability, capability; endurance, brooking; — bending, twisting (by heat); bend, twist, contortion; curling, curl; — (as last member of compounds), part. adj. Burning, inflaming, kindling; shining, irradiating; turning, twisting, folding
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever ( = tāp-tillī, q.v.s.v. tāp)
adj. Warm, hot, burning; shining, bright, luminous; twisted, waving, curling
s.m. A passage for light, opening in a wall; lattice, window; skylight
v.t. To inflame; to brighten, polish; to sharpen; to twist
(-ko), To be able to bear, to have patience (with, -par; syn. taḥammul ho-saknā)
adj. Of summer; like summer; pleasant as summer
(-kī), To muster strength, endurance, or courage, &c. (for); to be able to bear
adj. Bright, shining, brilliant, radiant; hot, passionate, choleric

ety: Prk. तेम्व or तेंव; S. तावत्
def: conj. Then, at that time; afterwards; thereupon; therefore, hence
subs: adv. Even then, then too; still, nevertheless, notwithstanding that
tab-talak, adv. Till then, up to that time; so long, so far
adv. Then indeed, then forsooth, then; whence, wherefore
adv. From that time forward, since that time; thence, thenceforward
adj. Of or belonging to that time
tab-hīṅ, tab-hūṅ, adv. (emphat. of tab), At that very time, just then; there and then, instantly; then indeed; therefore, for that same reason
adv. Only so long, just so long
adv. Then indeed; for that reason then
wety: From Sanskrit त (ta, “this; that”) + एवम् एव (evam eva, “just so”).

def: s.f. = tap, q.v. s.m. Warmth, heat, burning; fever (com. tap); affliction, pain, sorrow, distress; passion
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake ( = tāb-tillī)
s.m. Remover of sorrow or distress, an epithet of a deity.
wety: From Sanskrit त (ta, “this; that”) + एवम् एव (evam eva, “just so”).

ety: inf. n. of طيب 'to be or become pleasant,' &c.
def: adj. Good, excellent, pleasant, agreeable, delightful, sweet; pure, clean.

ety: inf. n. of طبع 'to seal, stamp, impress,' &c.
def: s.f. Stamping, printing; print, impression, edition (of a book, &c.); — nature, innate or natural disposition; genius; natural temper, temperament; idiosyncrasy; quality
subs: s.f. Trial of genius or of skill
adj. & s.m. Produced by the genius; original; invented; — a production of genius, an original invention
v.t. To stamp, impress; to print.

def: s.m. See تابه tāba.

ety: rel. n. from tāb, q.v.
def: s.m. A frying-pan; a skillet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تابه (tāba, “frying pan”), related to تاب (tāb, “heat”); cognate with Urdu توا (tavā).

ety: inf. n. v of بدل 'to change'
def: s.m. The becoming changed or altered; change, &c. = the next, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of t̤abīʻat, q.v.), Natures, temperaments, constitutions, dispositions, &c.

ety: Pehl. tavāh; Z. tapāh; S. tapasya
def: adj. Ruined, undone, spoiled, abject, wretched; bad, wicked, depraved
subs: adj. & adv. Ruined, &c.; — in wretched plight or condition, miserably poor or low.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَبَاه (tabāh).

def: adj. contrac. of tabāh, q.v.

ety: inf. n. v of بحر 'to enlarge,' &c.
def: s.m. Penetrating into the depths of science; being profoundly learned; — rolling in wealth.
s.f. Ruin, wreck, destruction, perdition; wretchedness, misery; wickedness, depravity
subs: v.n. To experience misery; to be afflicted.

def: adv. (prov) = tab-hī, q.v.s.v. tab.

ety: v.n. fr. طبق 'to stick or cleave to'; or from iv اطبق 'to cover'
def: s.m. A cover, lid; a dish, plate; a tray (esp. a round one = t̤abāq); a disc, an orb; a sphere, world; vault (of heaven); a leaf; gold-leaf, tin-foil, &c.; surface of the earth; layer, stratum; crust; coat (of an onion, &c.); one thing following another, or consecutive; story (of a house); a platform; class, order, degree.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَبَق (tabaq), borrowed from Arabic طَبَق (ṭabaq), from طَابَق (ṭābaq), borrowed from Middle Persian tʾp̄k', from Proto-Iranian *tāpaka-.

ety: prob. for A. t̤ibāq, pl. of t̤abaq, q.v.
def: s.m. A large dish or plate, a tray; a vessel for kneading dough in; a basin, bowl (syn. t̤asht).

ety: dim. of t̤abaq
def: s.m. A small plate; a saucer; a small tray, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of بختر 'to walk with an elegant gait'
def: s.m. Walking with a proud and self-conceited gait, strutting; inclining from side to side in walking.

ety: rel. n. fr. t̤abāq
def: adj. Of or relating to the plate or platter

ety: t̤abaq, q.v. + ṛī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) A small box; one of the bells or rattles that are let into the disc of a tambourine or timbrel.
s.m. One who plays on the t̤abla; a drummer.
s.m. One who plays on the t̤abla; a drummer.

ety: imperf. part. of tāftan; see tāb
def: adj. Hot, burning; light, luminous, shining, radiant, brilliant, resplendent, glittering.

s.m. A slap, blow ( = تماچه tamāća, q.v. s.m. Slap, box, blow

ety: imperf. part of tāftan; see tāb
def: part. adj. Bright, shining, luminous, sparkling
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تابنده (tābinda, tābanda).

ety: abst. s. fr. next, q.v.
def: s.f. Brightness, luminousness, lustre, splendour.
s.f. Light, brilliance, splendour.
s.m. A slap, blow ( = تماچه tamāća, q.v. s.m. Slap, box, blow

ety: inf. n. v of بني 'to build,' &c.
def: s.f. Adopting as a son; adoption.
s.m. People, nation, tribe, race, family, house, line.

ety: S. तरवारि?
def: s.m. A hatchet, an axe
subs: s.m. Axe-bearer, armed with an axe; a wood-cutter
adj. Curved like a tabar; hollow-backed (a horse)

ety: i.q. ḍābar, q.v.
def: s.m. A small lake, a pond, tank, pool (cf. ṭabbar; ḍabrā); — a hut, cottage (cf. ṭāpā, ṭāprā); family, household (cf. tabār).

ety: 6th conjn. of برك 'to become steady or firm,' &c.
def: adj. Hallowed; greatly to be magnified (used only in Ar. phrases, e.g. allāhǒ tabāraka wa taʻālā, God the hallowed or magnified and exalted).

ety: inf. n. v of برع 'to excel,' &c.
def: s.m. Giving supererogatorily; giving gratuitously; doing anything disinterestedly or voluntarily.

ety: inf. n. v of برك 'to kneel and lie down'
def: s.m. Looking for a blessing (by means of); auguring good (from); blessing, benediction; congratulation; a portion of presents (or what is left of food presented to great men, &c.) given to their dependants; sacred relics; — commutation for an offering incumbent on a religious mendicant holding an endowment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَبَرُّک (tabarruk), borrowed from Arabic تَبَرُّك (tabarruk), from تَبَرَّكَ (tabarraka). First attested in 1682 as Middle Hindi تبرک (tbrk /⁠tabarruk⁠/).

ety: acc. of tabarruk
def: adv. With the blessing of God; as a blessing; as a sacred relic.
s.m. pl. Benedictions; sacred relics; plenty; honours, dignities.
adv. One upon another, one after another, in succession, successively; repeatedly; in confusion or disorder, helter-skelter; immediately, straightway, forthwith.

ety: the Ar. form of P. تبرزد tabar-zad, 'what is chipped or cut with an axe or hatchet'; see tabar
def: s.m. Sugar-candy; loaf-sugar; fine hard white sugar; any crystallized substance; conserve of roses.

ety: S. तवक्षीर
def: s.f. Manna of bamboo (commonly t̤abāshīr; syn. baṅs-loćan).

ety: the Ar. form of P. تباشير tabāshīr = S. तवक्षीर
def: s.f. The sugar of the bamboo, bamboo-manna (a siliceous deposit in the joints of the bamboo; — syn. bans-loćan).

ety: inf. n. v. of بسم 'to smile'
def: s.m. Smiling; smile.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَبَسُّم (tabassum) from Arabic تَبَسُّم (tabassum).

ety: inf. n. v of بصر 'to see'
def: s.m. Descrying, considering with attention; sight, distinct perception; attentive consideration.

ety: inf. n. vi of بين 'to become separate'
def: s.m. Being separate or distinct; difference; opposition; contradiction; inconsistency; incommensurability (of two or more numbers).

ety: intens. n. fr. طبخ 'to cook'
def: s.m. A cook (cf. mat̤baḵẖ).
s.m. Family, household ( = ṭābar, q.v.). s.m. People, nation, tribe, race, family, house, line.

(-kī), To muster strength, endurance, or courage, &c. (for); to be able to bear
adj. Bright, shining, brilliant, radiant; hot, passionate, choleric

ety: inf. n. ii of بدل
def: s.f. Changing, altering; change, alteration, mutation, modification, substitution; transformation; transposition; — tabdīl karnā (-kī), To change, alter; to transfer, &c. (see badalnā)
subs: s.f. An unlawful or improper change or alteration; falsification
v.n. To be changed or altered; to be transferred
s.f. Change of appearance, disguise; personation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تبدیل (tabdīl), from Arabic تَبْدِيل (tabdīl).

def: s.f. Transfer (of officials, &c.); relief (of a guard).

ety: act. part. of تبغ 'to follow'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Following, dependent (on, -ke), subject (to, -ke), loyal, obedient, in the obedience (of), in submission (to); — follower, dependant, servant, slave; subordinate
subs: (common, though ungrammatical), adj. & s.m. Dependent; obedient, subject (to), submissive; — follower, dependant; servant, &c. ( = tāběʻ); — tāběʻ-dār banānā, tāběʻ-dār karnā, v.t. To make an adherent or follower (of), to make one submissive or obedient, to bring under control or subjection
s.f. Dependance; obedience, submission, control, subjection, service, allegiance, loyalty, fidelity
(-ke), To remail under subjection (to); to be submissive (to), &c.
